tr.td: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Human isophane insulin 70 unit/mL and recombinant human regular insulin 30 unit/mL solution for injection
Inject 10 units (0.1 mL) subcutaneously before breakfast and before dinner
1 tablet (325 mg) by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Acetaminophen 300 mg and codeine phosphate 15 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (300 mg/15 mg) by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed for pain. Do not exceed 6 tablets in 24 hours.
1 tablet (220 mg) by mouth every 12 hours as needed
Localized primary osteoarthritis of the hand
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth every 6 hours as needed
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Hepatitis A virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Depression screening using Patient Health Questionnaire Two-Item score (procedure)
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)
Localized primary osteoarthritis of the hand

ul.li: Reduce hand pain severity to mild or less as reported by patient
Improve ability to perform daily activities requiring hand use
Reduce duration of morning hand stiffness
Maintain hemoglobin A1c below 6.5% to reduce risk of diabetes progression.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce risk of kidney and cardiovascular complications.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight to support glycemic control and kidney health.
Reduce urine albumin excretion to less than 30 mg/g creatinine to slow kidney disease progression.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce hypertension-related risks.
Limit daily sodium intake to less than 2.3 grams to support blood pressure control.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight (BMI 18.5–24.9 kg/m2) to help control blood pressure.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity per week.
Limit alcohol intake to no more than 2 standard drinks per day for men.

tr.td: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Urea nitrogen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma

Glucose [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

Ketones [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

Protein [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.td: Localized primary osteoarthritis of the hand

ul.li: Reduce hand pain severity to mild or less as reported by patient
Improve ability to perform daily activities requiring hand use
Reduce duration of morning hand stiffness

display.value: Localized primary osteoarthritis of the hand

ul.li: Maintain hemoglobin A1c below 6.5% to reduce risk of diabetes progression.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce risk of kidney and cardiovascular complications.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight to support glycemic control and kidney health.
Reduce urine albumin excretion to less than 30 mg/g creatinine to slow kidney disease progression.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce hypertension-related risks.
Limit daily sodium intake to less than 2.3 grams to support blood pressure control.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight (BMI 18.5–24.9 kg/m2) to help control blood pressure.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity per week.
Limit alcohol intake to no more than 2 standard drinks per day for men.
